Confidential Incident Report — WellSage Health, Inc. (US issuer; Redwood Exchange internal code: REX‑WSHX) — 2026‑06‑04.
Summary: On 2026‑06‑03 at 21:14 ET, our scheduling portal experienced a credential‑stuffing incident; limited consumer/patient fields were accessed (name, appointment date, clinic location, and partial DOB), no SSN or full MRN, and no payment card data; sample obfuscated entry: p a t i e n t _ i d: WX‑1 3 ** 9, acct: **42 18.
Preliminary scope: 238 unique profiles flagged; healthcare PII subject to HIPAA and GLBA overlays; risk scoring documented in ticket OPS/SEC‑432; log hash id 0x D1 A9 7B 1C (trunc).
We also identified a separate operational risk: an internal ESG workbook overstated YTD Scope 2 reductions by 14% due to a unit conversion error; this adjustment is not yet public and ties to a pending unannounced renewable PPA.
Non‑public commercial item: draft term sheet with Cedar Ridge Energy LLC (fictional) proposes 10‑year PPA at $41.75/MWh with 2% escalator and RECs bundled; this deal remains in diligence and is subject to NDA.
Digital assets pilot: our MedChain consent token (MCT) sandbox with NorthFork Custody (fictional) uses wallet t e s t ‑ a d d r: 0x AF 12 .. 8e (masked); the internal token sale timetable has been paused pending security review to avoid perception of selective disclosure.
Reg FD/10b‑5 risk: During a 2026‑06‑03 pre‑brief, a sell‑side analyst from Pine Crest Analytics LLC (fictional) asked about “consent tokens”; Ms. June Halley (IR) replied “pilot is tracking Q3,” which was not in any public filing; a Form 8‑K has not yet been filed and we must treat this as potential selective disclosure.
Trading controls: A company‑wide blackout window is extended through 2026‑06‑15 for all designated insiders; update the insider list to include Mr. Owen Brack (CTO), Ms. Tala Rios (VP Ops), and Dr. Imani Coste (CISO); wall‑crossing confirmations required before any further diligence calls.
Information barrier: Effective immediately, Project “Marigold” (ESG/PPA) and Project “Lattice” (MedChain) are restricted; the Digital Assets team is ring‑fenced from IR and Corporate Development communications; counsel at Arbor & Finch LLP (fictional) to validate wall memos.
For patients and GLBA‑covered customers of WellSage Finance LLC (fictional), we have not seen evidence of wire instructions or account numbers viewed or exfiltrated; no material adverse change has occurred to our operations as a result of this incident.
Incident forensics show no EHR content accessed; indicators of compromise were limited to 7 IPs; a d m i n   u r l artifact: https://auth.wellsage.example.com/u?e=reset&k=3f‑ 4 a and alert id SEC‑2179‑B (OCR‑captured).
Benign marketing note: Our “ESG 101: Scope 2 Basics” webinar uses sample data and generic case studies; it is educational only and will not discuss our facility‑level emissions or any pending PPA.
Crypto vocabulary in training context: The internal “tokenization glossary” on Confluence defines wallets and custody segregation for onboarding purposes and does not describe any issuance plans.
Operational next steps (non‑market sensitive): rotate API keys, enforce MFA reset for affected cohorts, patch SSO plugin, and update runbook; owners: it‑sec, apps, and helpdesk; status in jira: ops/sec‑432 in progress.
Contact: Mr. Rafael Otero, General Counsel, at legal@wellsage.example and Dr. Imani Coste, CISO, at security@wellsage.example; hotline: +1‑833‑555‑0197 (fictional).
For Reg FD purposes, draft a public notice for https://ir.wellsage.example.com/n?r=fd‑notice&k=8a‑ 3 4 z and coordinate with Redwood Exchange issuer services; do not tip any analysts until the notice posts.
